chapter: JEF P104 – A Question of Trust
title: A Question of Trust – Easy LAQs

questions.question: Describe the character of Horace Danby.
questions.answer: Horace Danby is a respectable locksmith who secretly steals once a year. He loves rare books and believes he is not a typical criminal. Though intelligent and careful, his misplaced trust leads to his downfall.

questions.question: Why did Horace Danby steal every year?
questions.answer: Horace stole once every year to collect money for buying rare and expensive books. He justified his crime by limiting it and planning carefully.

questions.question: Describe the setting of the story ‘A Question of Trust’.
questions.answer: The story is mainly set at Shotover Grange, a big house where Horace plans a robbery. The quiet setting and absence of servants help Horace feel confident.

questions.question: Explain how Horace planned the robbery.
questions.answer: Horace studied the house carefully, observed the servants’ routine, examined the wiring, and prepared tools in advance. His planning made him confident.

questions.question: Who was the young lady and what role did she play in the story?
questions.answer: The young lady pretended to be the owner’s wife. She cleverly deceived Horace and made him open the safe, leading to his capture.

questions.question: Describe the meeting between Horace and the young lady.
questions.answer: Horace meets a calm and confident young lady inside the house. Believing her to be the owner’s wife, he trusts her and follows her instructions.

questions.question: How did the young lady deceive Horace Danby?
questions.answer: She pretended to forget the safe’s combination and threatened to call the police, forcing Horace to open the safe.

questions.question: What mistake did Horace make during the robbery?
questions.answer: Horace removed his gloves to light the young lady’s cigarette and left fingerprints on the safe, which led to his arrest.

questions.question: Why was Horace Danby arrested in the end?
questions.answer: Horace was arrested because his fingerprints were found on the safe at Shotover Grange.

questions.question: Explain the irony in Horace Danby’s life.
questions.answer: Horace made locks for a living but broke them while stealing. In prison, he became an assistant librarian, gaining books but losing freedom.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: The main theme of the story is trust and deception. Horace’s misplaced trust leads to his downfall.

questions.question: Why is the title ‘A Question of Trust’ appropriate?
questions.answer: The title is appropriate because Horace’s blind trust in the young lady causes his failure.

questions.question: Describe Horace Danby’s life in prison.
questions.answer: In prison, Horace worked as an assistant librarian. Though surrounded by books, he lost his freedom.

questions.question: What role does Sherry play in the story?
questions.answer: Sherry, the dog, creates a sense of safety and normalcy, making Horace feel confident.

questions.question: Explain Horace Danby’s love for books.
questions.answer: Horace loved rare books deeply. His passion for books motivated his crimes.

questions.question: Why did Horace believe he was not a real criminal?
questions.answer: He believed so because he stole only from the rich and did not use violence.

questions.question: Explain the importance of planning in Horace’s robberies.
questions.answer: Planning helped Horace avoid danger and succeed for many years.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches that crime leads to punishment and blind trust is dangerous.

questions.question: Describe the young lady’s character.
questions.answer: She is intelligent, confident, deceptive, and morally ruthless.

questions.question: Why is the ending of the story ironic?
questions.answer: A clever thief is deceived by another thief and punished for his crime.

questions.question: How does trust become a weakness for Horace?
questions.answer: Horace’s trust in the young lady makes him careless and leads to his capture.

questions.question: Explain the idea of ‘honour among thieves’ in the story.
questions.answer: The story proves that honour among thieves is a myth.

questions.question: Why did Horace fear going to prison?
questions.answer: He feared prison because he would lose freedom and access to books.

questions.question: What human weakness caused Horace’s downfall?
questions.answer: Misplaced trust and overconfidence caused his downfall.

questions.question: Summarise the story ‘A Question of Trust’.
questions.answer: The story narrates how a clever thief named Horace Danby is deceived by a young lady due to misplaced trust and is finally arrested.
